compliance_disclaimer: This is an automated control mapping for Solutions Architect planning. It is informational only and is NOT legal, audit, or compliance advice. Confirm all controls with the institution's compliance, legal, and internal-audit teams before relying on them.

SOX.name: Sarbanes-Oxley Act
mainframe_impact: Financial reporting systems must maintain audit trails during migration
Change management controls must be documented
Data integrity must be provably maintained

controls.intent: Immutable audit trail of all changes to financial-reporting systems
Documented, segregated change management for financial systems
Provable data integrity through migration (source↔target reconciliation)

migration_considerations: Maintain parallel systems during transition for audit continuity
Document all data transformation rules
Implement automated reconciliation between source and target
Ensure segregation of duties in IAM policies

PCI_DSS.name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
mainframe_impact: Cardholder data must remain encrypted during migration
Network segmentation must be maintained
Access controls must be equivalent or stronger

controls.intent: Render PAN unreadable at rest (encryption / tokenization)
Network segmentation of the cardholder data environment
Detect and alert on threats to the CDE

migration_considerations: Tokenize cardholder data before migration
Use AWS PrivateLink for data transfer
Implement network segmentation via VPC design
Maintain PCI audit scope documentation

FDIC.name: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation Guidelines
mainframe_impact: Core banking data availability requirements
Disaster recovery and business continuity
Third-party risk management for cloud providers

controls.intent: Documented RPO/RTO with tested multi-AZ/multi-Region DR
Cloud-provider due diligence and exit strategy

migration_considerations: Document cloud provider due diligence
Implement RPO/RTO requirements in AWS architecture
Maintain data residency requirements
Plan for regulatory examination readiness

OCC.name: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
OCC.applies_to: National banks and federal savings associations
mainframe_impact: Technology risk management during migration
Operational resilience requirements
Third-party relationship management

controls.intent: Centralized security posture and vulnerability management

migration_considerations: Conduct technology risk assessment before migration
Implement operational resilience testing
Document exit strategy from cloud provider
Maintain board-level reporting on migration progress

GLBA.name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
mainframe_impact: Customer financial data privacy during migration
Safeguards Rule compliance
Information sharing controls

controls.intent: Protect customer NPI with encryption + access control
Audit access to customer financial data

migration_considerations: Classify all customer data before migration
Implement data loss prevention controls
Maintain privacy notices and opt-out mechanisms
Encrypt all customer data in transit and at rest

FFIEC.name: Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Guidance
FFIEC.applies_to: US depository institutions and their technology service providers
mainframe_impact: Architecture, infrastructure, and operations examination expectations
Business continuity and resilience management
Authentication and access management controls

controls.intent: Resilience and recovery aligned to business impact analysis

migration_considerations: Map controls to FFIEC IT Examination Handbook booklets
Maintain evidence packages for examination readiness
Document resilience testing against the business impact analysis

FINRA_17a-4.name: SEC Rule 17a-4 / FINRA Books-and-Records
mainframe_impact: Records must be retained in non-rewriteable, non-erasable (WORM) form
Retention periods and audit-trail of records must be preserved
Designated third-party access to records on request

controls.intent: Store required records in WORM form for the mandated retention period
Audit-trail and designated-examiner access to retained records

migration_considerations: Confirm WORM retention is enforced at the storage layer, not the application
Document the retention schedule per record type
Validate examiner-access procedures post-migration

general_best_practices: Conduct regulatory impact assessment before migration planning
Engage compliance team early in the modernization process
Maintain dual-run capability during transition periods
Implement automated compliance monitoring from day one
Document all architectural decisions with compliance rationale
Use AWS Artifact to obtain SOC 2, PCI, and ISO reports
Implement least-privilege access across all migrated workloads
Plan for regulatory examination and audit readiness
